title: Fermented Arabic Rice Stir-fry
description: This dish combines the silky texture of fermented rice with aromatic spices and a quick stir-fry method for a light and vibrant meal.
instructions: Rinse the Arabic Rice under cold water to remove excess starch. Rinse twice more, then soak in hot water for about 1 hour.
After soaking, drain and transfer rice into a pot with enough water (about 2 cups per cup of rice). Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer until the water is absorbed and the rice is tender.
While the rice simmers, chop garlic and onions finely. If using peppers or carrots, slice them thinly.
Heat olive oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Add garlic, onions, and optional peppers/carrots. Stir fry for about 2 minutes until vegetables start to soften.
Stir the cooked rice into the vegetable mixture and season with salt and pepper. Mix well to combine all ingredients evenly.
Add sesame seeds if desired for a nutty flavor. Cook for another minute to allow flavors to meld together.
Serve immediately as a side dish or as an appetizer.
